dr:
DELTARUNE | UNDERTALE

i've been a fan of UT since it's release, originally watching jacksepticeye's playthrough.
ever since then it inspired me to do many things and taught me to always be kind and to power on.
this and DR are games that i hold dear in my heart, and i am looking forward to the conclusion of DR in the future.

10/10
uk:
ULTRAKILL

i feel it'd be silly to say that this game made me pick up reading, but it really did.
after finding out ULTRAKILL was based on Dante's "Divine Comedy", i've been wanting to read it for a while now.
whilst i still haven't done that, i've started reading a few other books, so it's still something i'd say.
other than that, this is also my favourite game of all time. great mechanics, story, OST and so on so forth.

10/10
cp2077:
Cyberpunk 2077

at first i was quite hesitant on trying it out, but the game grew really quick on me, and i ended up doing 100% in it.
it is one of those few games that are great to play when you're not feeling well in the head.
it contains many thoughtful moments, a vibrant world and amazing storytelling.
whilst not up there with the rest of my games, it is most definitely a game i'd recommend to everyone.

9/10
p3r:
Persona 3 Reload

similar to cyberpunk, i never actually played anything similar to persona, but i got into it really quickly.
the characters pulled me in immediately, and i fell in love with the game.
there were so many little moments that made me laugh, and plenty that were quite philosophical.
i've yet to play the rest of the persona games, but i'm sure they are as great as P3R is.

9/10
hitman:
HITMAN

considering i only played WoA, i don't really know how much it differs from the legacy series.
regardless, this is a phenomenal game and one of the only that allow you to do practically anything.
the story is well made and i thoroughly enjoyed it. it is also great that they're adding content up to this day as well, even if i don't play it often.

9/10
doom:
DOOM

i hadn't really played an fast-paced action shooter game that also had it's own story before ( either that or i've forgotten ).
DOOM (2016) and DOOM Eternal was a pleseant surpise to say the least, and later i bought the full franchise ( yet to play the older games though ).
it had a few inconveniences here and there, but i still had a great time with it.

8/10
hl:
Half-Life

i've not much to say about this one. the original is actually my favourite game of all time, for many reasons.
one being that i played it since i even started gaming, and that i imagine the world wouldn't be the same without it.
have not played half life alyx yet, but i plan on doing so sometime.

10/10
terraria:
Terraria

this was one of the first games i've ever bought on steam, and it holds a special place in my heart.
while i've only played about ~300 hours of it, i'm very happy with how much i've played it.
the developers behind the game are also just amazing, considering they've done the "final update" for a few times in a row now.

8/10
hk:
Hollow Knight | Silksong

i wasn't sure if i would've enjoyed the game when i first bought it, but i got really into it really quickly. safe to say, i am a huge fan now.
hollow knight and silksong are quite difficult to get into, but it'd be a shame to miss out on these games.
no game has given me such a challenge as its 100% and i am proud to have done it.

10/10
m2033:
Metro Franchise

it is a really great game, and i was quite taken aback by it. whilst i wasn't too much of a fan of the gameplay, the story was fascinating and i've had my fill with it.

7/10
rdr:
Red Dead Redemption 2

absolutely amazing game. similar to cyberpunk and persona 3, it teaches you a bit about life, but in many different ways.
the gameplay, story and graphics we're all amazing. the only downside is that it was developed by R*, so it never saw anything than the base story it has.

8/10
tetris:
Tetris

maybe this is a bit broad, but all the tetris games are simply just very fun.
Puyo Puyo Tetris, Tetris Effect, and TETR.IO, i do enjoy those games a lot and it's a great time no matter what.
i won't be giving a rating for this cause it is not quite specific so i'll just leave my thoughts here.
portal:
Portal

simply a classic. both 1 and 2 have their moments, and are one of the better puzzle games that exist today.
i haven't much more to say about it, but i recommend it to everyone, ESPECIALLY the co-op of Portal 2.

8/10
hades:
Hades

considering it's a rougelike, it actually had a quite intriguing story.
unfortunately because it is a rougelike, it might not appeal to everyone, but it is a great time nonetheless. i even ended up getting all the achivements in it ( which was a bit tedious ).

8/10
balatro:
Balatro

dare i say one of the better rougelikes released in this day and age.
it is actually criminal how much time you can get out of this game. i've been playing for at least 100 hours and i barely scratched the surface of the completion. all i would wish for is to get more updates, but i'm not too bummed out about it.
in short, it is a great time killer and when you just want to have a small gaming session.

9/10
bmth:
Bring Me The Horizon

one of my favourite bands ever. even since i started listening to them, they grew really quick to be my favourite.
i love all their albums, from Count Your Blessings to amo and the Post Human albums, i love them in every aspect and will keep doing so on the road ahead.

Favourite Track : DArkSide

Favourite Album : That's The Spirit
lp:
Linkin Park

classic band. i fear there's not much to say about them.
i've listened to them when i was quite young, but never got into their music until recently. safe to say, they've stuck with me for a while now.
all their albums are masterpieces and they rarely ever miss. R.I.P. Chester

Favourite Track : Two Faced

Favourite Album : LIVING THINGS
tdg:
Three Days Grace

a band that i've only picked up recently actually. safe to say i don't regret it.
their tracks stick to me quite a lot. they have iconic lyrics and amazing instrumentals. safe to say they're one of my more favourite bands.

Favourite Track : I Hate Everything About You

Favourite Album : One-X
bb:
Bitbreaker

whilst a relatively new, i am very taken aback as to how well they make their tracks.
i'm not too much a fan of vocaloids, but in their tracks it is very surprising how well they tune them to fit the metal/rock genre.
definitely will be looking foward to hearing from from them.

Favourite Track : You Make Me Sick
ado:
Ado

i have fallen in love with ado ever since i heard her in usseewa. the raw energy coming out of her just scratches my brain.
her vocal range is so wide it genuinely surprised me. whilst i'm not too much of pop music, she is a exception and will always have a place in my music library.

Favourite Track : Usseewa
klk:
Kill la Kill

actually one of my favourite animes of all time. i loved the loud ost and the characters are written great.
while i do agree that it has a quite a bit of fan service, it didn't particulary bother me and it was still a great watch for me nonetheless.

10/10
gl:
Gurren Lagann

one of the few animes that actually made me cry a few times. it is filled with so many exciting and great moments, and a handful of sad moments.
the characters in this anime are simply amazing, and i loved each and every one of them. i would recommend this to anyone since it's simply such a wonderful anime.

9/10
frieren:
Frieren : Beyond Journey's End

the premmise of the anime is fairly simple, but it is very well written and i enjoyed what it had to offer so far.
it got me so intrigued that ended up picking up on the manga.
safe to say, the anime hasn't dissapointed me and i'm looking forward to it's conclusion.

10/10
bleach:
BLEACH

this is one of my top animes i must say. the story is both fun and serious, and the animation is just simply fascinating.
the only thing that bothered me really is the long episode count of the original, but i still powered on and watched through the entire thing ( except fillers ).
to put it simply, i can't wait for the continuation of the TYBW.

9/10
er:
Cyberpunk : Edgerunners

this anime originally got me into the cyberpunk franchise ( like many others watchers ).
it is so wonderfully done in all ascepts and it left me with a hole in my heart. would recommend it to almost anyone.
i am very much looking forward to the 2nd season of it too.

8/10
sg:
Steins;Gate | 0

not sure where i'd even begin with this one. it might just be one of the best animes ever made to be honest, and it shows.
i like how many intricate things are connected with each other in this anime. the characters are also really funny and amazingly written.
safe to say, i don't think any other anime reaches to it personally.

10/10
dnd:
Dungeons & Dragons

whilst i didn't really ever experience a full campaign in any of my D&D sessions, i think it is a really great game nonetheless.
considering the fact the only boundry is your imagination, you don't have to explicity follow the rules of it, and i think that's pretty great.
overall, very fun game if you're looking for something more social.
monopoly:
Monopoly

despite how annoying and rage inducing monopoly can actually be, i actually love it a lot.
even if it does take quite a while to finish a full game of it, i think it has its charm and can make for some great moments when playing with family and friends. one of the top board games up there for sure.
codenames:
Codenames

i've only played the original version of it, and i've got to say, it is pretty damn good.
it's one of those games if you don't really have much time but still want to play something with people. it is also a great way to actually learn more of a language as well. if you have a wide vocabulary, it only gives a better experience.
dixit:
Dixit

whilst i haven't exactly played it much, it is a very fun game nonetheless.
playing this game goes to show how different the people around you are and what they've experienced. if you have the time for a more lengthy gaming session, this is one of those games to play multiple rounds for.
uno:
Uno

very classic game, i've not much to say about this one. it can be fun, make you go insane and also make you feel quite strategic at times. i actually own multiple versions of it so i'll quickly list them down.

Uno Flip!

very fun twist on the original game, having two sided cards makes for a very unique experience and i recommend it.

Uno Teams

not a bad edition of the game, though it can get quite lengthy. i recommend playing it and changing the rules so it fits your squad.

Uno No Mercy

i both love and hate this one with a passion. it is VERY brutal, you and your friends WILL hate each other, and it'll give you MANY moments that'll be remembered.
